# TDEC Documentation For LLMs

TDEC means Top Debate Engine Championship. It runs structured debates between
LLMs and scores them with independent judge LLMs.

Start here:

- README.md - short project overview and common commands.
- docs/tutorials/first-tournament.md - hands-on beginner path.
- docs/how-to/ - task-oriented recipes.
- docs/reference/ - exact command, configuration, and artifact descriptions.
- docs/explanation/ - design rationale and scoring model.

Key source files:

- src/tdec/cli.py - Click command line entry point.
- src/tdec/config.py - YAML config dataclasses and validation.
- src/tdec/debate.py - turn sequencing and model pairings.
- src/tdec/judging.py - judge prompt execution and JSON repair.
- src/tdec/models.py - LiteLLM calls, metrics, cost extraction, metadata capture.
- src/tdec/tournament.py - tournament orchestration, thread pool, summaries, Elo.
- src/tdec/artifacts.py - run directory, compact/full artifact writing.
- src/tdec/prompts.py - debater and judge prompt templates.

Important behavior:

- Self-debates are enabled by default. Set `run.include_self_debates: false`
  to run only cross-model pairings.
- `run.workers` controls concurrent debate and judgement jobs. The CLI
  `--workers` option overrides it.
- Provider failures skip the failed debate or judgement and write error records
  to `errors/`; they do not stop the full tournament.
- Artifacts are compact by default. Use `--artifact-verbosity full` to keep full
  raw response metadata for every model call.
- Blank model outputs always keep full response metadata so the failure can be
  diagnosed after the run.
- Missing API key environment variables fail before the tournament starts.

Documentation style:

- Tutorials teach by doing, with minimal explanation.
- How-to guides solve one named task.
- Reference pages describe exact fields and commands.
- Explanation pages discuss design tradeoffs.
